And it's a lot of really heavy psychological mind games. And one of the big ones is like silence and getting them to speak and this and that. So there's silence where the other person is maybe just holding frame, but it's not awkward, right? That's okay. They said, especially when it comes to service based sales. So for me, when I actually want the other person's talk or when I want them to ask, you know, more about the service or how do we move forward? I'll just write stuff down. Next thing is not boxing yourself in. So ladies and gentlemen, always go for open-ended questions rather than direct questions because direct questions box yourself in. Open-ended questions allow you to keep the flow of the conversation. For example, let's say you're speaking to a prospect rather than asking them, hey, just next Tuesday at 4 p.m. work for you. Instead go, what does the rest of your week look like? Or, hey, what does the early next week look like for you? Right, because that's more open-ended. You know, whereas if you ask a specific day, it's like a yes or no. Whereas if you ask for like a more general timeframe, then instead that gives them more space to then come in and tell you, oh, this works, this time works, this time won't work, et cetera, et cetera. Same thing with like, you know, if you ask them a question of like, why don't you just run the ads yourself? Or why do you even wanna grow your business?
